relatedFeatures.reason: Start with file-scoped namespaces, then add file-local types when helper types should stay file-private.
Pair file-local types with top-level programs to keep tiny app entry files organized without exporting helper types.
Use primary constructors with file-local helper types when small immutable collaborators belong in one file.
Combine file-local scoping and records to model implementation-only data without widening your public API.

examples.title: Declaring helper types with the file modifier
examples.description: File-local types keep implementation details in the same file while preventing those types from leaking into project-wide namespaces.

examples.title: From nested private classes to file-local collaborators
examples.description: Move deeply nested helper types out of containing classes while keeping them hidden from other files through the file modifier.
